card.title: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
card.summary: Unify phone, browser, and installed-app geometry so menu, play, overlays, touch controls, status, and maze framing share measured safe-content rules.
card.objective: Resolve the operator's physical-iPhone spacing, control-feel, maze-fit, and timer findings through shared geometry and deterministic input contracts rather than screenshot offsets.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu, Options, play, and Pause remain contained without overlap, clipping, or wasteful dead space.
One finger can slide continuously across the D-pad, and the stick has a lower position, wider throw, stable cardinal intent, and clean diagonal behavior.
The phone maze border remains edge-tight while every maze size scales its inner tiles to fill the stable frame.
Player and AI timers stop on goal arrival and remain frozen during completion lifecycle transitions.
The exact merged production build passes fresh physical-iPhone and installed-PWA review.
discoveries: Fresh physical-iPhone production screenshots exposed remaining spacing, control-feel, maze-frame, and timer defects after PR #75.
Draft PR #76 now carries the complete follow-up at commit 5765c2725221d734d6eca43a2c698dd200968072.
nextActions: Review and merge PR #76 only while its controls, timer, maze-frame, and visual gates remain green.
Deploy the exact reviewed merge only under a new explicit Mazer production approval.
Collect fresh physical-iPhone Safari and installed-PWA screenshots plus arrow/stick feel feedback.
blockers: The latest packet is not yet merged or deployed, so new physical-device production acceptance remains pending.
evidence: Exact 390x844 DPR-3 surface matrix: 39/39 passed with zero console messages and zero page errors.
Arrow live route reached the goal in 139/139 moves; stick live route reached the goal in 82/82 moves; both timers remained frozen on resample.

entry.headline: Physical-iPhone controls, timer, and maze-frame follow-up is proof-backed on draft PR #76
completed: Added continuous same-pointer D-pad retargeting and recoverable center-gap behavior.
Moved the stick lower, widened knob travel, reduced the deadzone, strengthened cardinal hysteresis, and accelerated retarget cadence.
Made the phone maze frame edge-tight and maze-size independent while scaling inner tiles to fill it.
Froze player and AI elapsed time at exact goal arrival and exposed two-sample runtime proof.
discovered: The prior fixed phone tile cadence caused smaller maze dimensions to underfill a stable border.
Moderate stick diagonal noise needed a higher secondary-direction threshold to preserve intended cardinal travel.
next: Review and merge the draft PR, then obtain a new explicit production approval.
Repeat the operator's physical-iPhone menu, play, Pause, arrows, stick, and end-timer checks against that exact production build.
blockers: Production/device acceptance cannot be claimed before the draft head is merged and explicitly released.

card.title: normalize browser UI layout across viewports
card.summary: Normalize responsive browser geometry so maze, menu, overlay, and control layouts derive from the active content rectangle without replaying stale viewport dimensions.
card.objective: Keep one tight phone maze frame across maze sizes while scaling its inner tile cadence from current viewport and maze data.
acceptanceCriteria: No viewport-derived geometry is persisted across incompatible browser states.
The phone maze border keeps a stable edge-tight margin across supported maze dimensions.
Inner maze tiles scale to fill the stable border without clipping or escaping it.
Menu, Options, play, Pause, and dialogs remain contained through supported viewport transitions.
discoveries: A fixed phone tile cadence made the border size depend on maze dimensions and visibly underfilled smaller mazes.
The revised 8px outer-frame contract passed exact phone surface proof without persisting geometry.
nextActions: Merge and explicitly release the exact PR #76 maze-frame revision.
Verify multiple maze dimensions on physical iPhone keep the same tight border and fill it cleanly.
Retain native maximize, restore, and browser-chrome automation as a separate open proof gate.
blockers: Physical-iPhone acceptance of the new draft build and native desktop window-transition automation remain open.
evidence: At 390px width, tested maze sizes keep a 374px board at x=8 through x=382 while inner tile size changes with maze dimension.
Exact 390x844 DPR-3 surface matrix passed 39/39 with zero console messages and zero page errors.

entry.headline: Maze-size-independent phone framing is implemented and route-proven
completed: Replaced fixed phone tile cadence with one stable edge-tight board frame.
Derived inner tile size from stable board size, safe inset, and active maze dimension.
Verified the full phone surface family after the layout change.
discovered: The outer border and inner maze scale must be independent variables for consistent physical-phone composition.
next: Review and merge PR #76, then collect physical-device multi-size proof after an explicitly approved release.
blockers: The revision is not yet in canonical production.

card.title: shared menu and play run status panel
card.summary: Use one two-row status component in menu AI and active play for time, contextual rank, score out of 100, and maze level.
card.objective: Make the displayed player and AI elapsed time stop at exact goal arrival and share that frozen duration with completion telemetry.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu and play consume the same status-panel layout and formatting contract.
Player and AI timers freeze immediately when the end tile is reached.
The frozen HUD value remains unchanged during goal hold, deconstruction, and handoff.
Completion telemetry uses the same frozen elapsed duration.
Displayed score remains traceable to the canonical run-quality contract.
discoveries: The prior HUD computed elapsed time from the current frame even after goal arrival.
PR #76 records exact player and AI completion timestamps before lifecycle reset or telemetry recording.
nextActions: Merge and explicitly release the PR #76 timer lifecycle correction.
Verify player and AI timers on physical iPhone stop on the end tile and remain unchanged through goal hold.
Confirm final canonical score-field authority before terminal completion.
blockers: The timer correction is not yet in canonical production; physical-device acceptance and score authority remain open.
evidence: Arrow live QA reached the goal in 139/139 moves and retained 12,818ms on a 96ms resample.
Stick live QA reached the goal in 82/82 moves and retained 21,376ms on a 96ms resample.
Timer helper, scene diagnostics, and live-QA timer gates are covered by focused tests and the passing production build.

entry.headline: Player and AI goal timers now freeze at exact arrival
completed: Captured player and AI completion timestamps at end-tile arrival.
Routed HUD and completion telemetry through one frozen elapsed-time resolver.
Added runtime timer diagnostics and a mandatory two-sample live-QA freeze gate.
discovered: A visual timer can continue after movement stops unless goal arrival, HUD formatting, and telemetry share one completion timestamp.
next: Review and merge PR #76, explicitly release it, and repeat end-tile timing proof on physical iPhone.
blockers: Canonical production still serves the previous release.
